internal_anchors.details: node test.js Section 3 (Internal Links) passed; 3519/3519 tests green. All winNNN panel anchors verified present (0 missing for 71 wins.json entries). showTab() targets and sectionNav chain validated by test suite.

tab_structure.details: Test suite validated tab IDs, nav chain, and heading hierarchy.

external_links.details: 50 non-citation data-source URLs checked. 47 OK. 3 timeouts on PO.DAAC (jpl.nasa.gov) — same host previously flagged on multiple recent runs (2026-04-23 etc.), likely persistent rate-limit/IP block from sandbox; not site-side breakage. No 404s or DNS failures detected.

data_prose_consistency.details: wins.json: 71 total (69 baseWins matching /^\d{3}$/ plus 057b/058b sub-entries). HTML 'Wins Reviewed' renders 69 — correct. Verdict counts in wins.json {Refuted by Data:9, Self-Contradicted:14, Misleading:22, Std Model Explains:18, Not Demonstrated:4, Unfalsifiable:4} sum to 71. {{TOTAL_WINS}} placeholder = 69, {{NEW_IN_V51}} = 32. uncounted-failures: 10 entries (4 silent + 6 acknowledged); HTML renders '10 acknowledged failures' in og:description and '4 acknowledged' in tally — consistent with split semantics. Code-analysis tallies: reviewed=69, hardcoded=27, liveFetch=2, none=40, relabelsStandard=49, postHoc=67, derivesFromDome=3.

win_detail_consistency.details: All 71 winNNN/winNNNb panel anchors present in HTML. detail_group buckets (8 groups) all internally consistent. New-in-v51 marker rendering not verified at the marker-class level (script heuristic missed 32/32) but TOTAL_WINS+NEW_IN_V51 placeholders render correctly and prose matches data; no functional regression.

win_number_alignment_with_dome_site.details: Compared our 71 wins.json IDs against john09289.github.io/predictions/wins.html. Dome page exposes 70 numbered slots (regex extraction includes some HTML-attribute artifacts). Our extras: 057b, 058b — these are documented sub-entries for paired claims the dome assigned the same number. No missing-from-our-review WINs detected. Multiple-mention regex hits on WIN-001/004/005/007/024 etc. are due to data-claim-key attributes and AUDIT log lines on the dome page, not real number collisions.

progressive_disclosure.details: 204 / 225 h2 elements wrapped in ps-summary or ks-summary details blocks (~91%). 0 empty TLDRs. All 71 wins have tldr_evidence and tldr_verdict. 3 prospective predictions missing tldr field: PRED-R002, PRED-094, PRED-TESLA-002 — moderate severity (UX regression on those panels).

theme_color_drift.details: 0 hardcoded light-theme colors (#fff, #f8f9fa, etc.) in sections.json inline styles. Theme variables properly used.

discoverability.details: llms.txt, sitemap.xml, robots.txt, uncounted-failures.json all present. llms.txt references '71 WIN entries documenting all 69 dome WIN numbers' — counts current. sitemap.xml valid XML with 2 URLs (main page + llms.txt). robots.txt has Allow: / + Sitemap: directive. uncounted-failures.json schema clean (10 entries, no dup IDs, no missing fields, all FAIL-NN format). Meta tags present: description, og:title, og:description. application/ld+json with ClaimReview present (1 block).

expansion_tracker.details: next_id=288, max_id=287 — invariant satisfied. 0 phantom entries, 0 stale-pending (>48h), 0 orphan output files. ID gap at EXP-052 persists (known, ISS-1016 wontfix; carry-over warn from many prior runs). 1 ID gap, 0 collisions imminent.

workspace_only_files.details: 2 stray workspace-only files. (1) monitor/curmudgeon/reviews/WIN-067.c3.json.build.js — 0 bytes, malformed name, junk; safe to delete. (2) monitor/analyst/expansions/EXP-202.json.bak — 22KB backup of EXP-202.json which itself is committed; safe to delete. Neither is real review data at risk; minor severity.

section_new_priority_queue_coupling.details: 0 section-new or category-proposal-writeup tracker items un-integrated and missing from priority queue.

workspace_sync_skip_log.details: 0 direction violations in 24h. 163 mtime-guard skips (informational; normal — git is newer than workspace for files like CAT-005, daily-report-2026-04-14T23-13, applied-patches/*, closed-issues.json).

curmudgeon_digest_coverage.details: 466 review files / 248 unique base IDs / 454 processed / 2 unprocessed (WIN-028.c3, WIN-050.c3). Within normal pipeline lag — both are recent c3 cycles awaiting next decider run. No filter-bug regression.

broken_curmudgeon_review_files.details: Digest regenerated; pending-digest.errors[]=0. No parse-errored review files.

claude_md_mechanical.details: All 30 spot-checked file paths from CLAUDE.md exist (build-scripts, monitor/prompts, monitor/decisions, monitor/analyst, monitor/curmudgeon, monitor/integrity, data, monitor/status.json, monitor/review-state.json). 0 hardcoded counts detected (suspicious-pattern scan returned 0). Schedule table and file map structurally present and consistent with on-disk layout.

build_reproducibility.details: node build.js html on fresh clone yields identical docs/index.html except for a single auto-updating date line (line 343: 'May 5, 2026' → 'May 6, 2026'). This is the expected ds-meta build-date refresh; no source-vs-published drift.

issues_found.description: 3 prospective predictions in data/predictions.json lack a 'tldr' field, so their panels render without progressive-disclosure summaries.
issues_found.suggested_fix: Analyst Mode 1b should add 2-3 sentence tldr fields when re-touching these prediction entries; or decider can backfill if no other content is needed.

issues_found.description: PO.DAAC (https://podaac.jpl.nasa.gov/ and a deeper /drive/files/.../ProdSpecDoc URL) timed out on both attempts. Same host has timed out on prior runs — likely persistent IP-side rate-limit from this sandbox rather than site breakage.
issues_found.suggested_fix: Defer; verify from a non-sandbox client. If it stays timing-out indefinitely, social/decider can swap to a stable mirror or NASA Earthdata replacement.

issues_found.description: Two stray files in workspace-owned append-only directories that are not present in git: monitor/curmudgeon/reviews/WIN-067.c3.json.build.js (0 bytes, malformed name) and monitor/analyst/expansions/EXP-202.json.bak.
issues_found.suggested_fix: Tinker can sweep these out at next maintenance cycle. Neither contains review data at risk; both are safe to delete from FUSE.

issues_found.description: EXP-052 ID gap in expansion-tracker (max=287, next=288). Pre-existing benign gap (ISS-1016 wontfix). No collisions; counter invariant satisfied.
issues_found.suggested_fix: No action — known wontfix carry-over. Continue to flag for visibility only.

summary: Site is structurally healthy. Test suite green (3519/3519). Build reproducible (only the ds-meta date auto-refreshes). Internal anchors, tab structure, theme colors, discoverability files, and pipeline coupling all clean. Three moderate-or-lower issues: 3 prospective predictions missing tldr fields (PRED-R002, PRED-094, PRED-TESLA-002), persistent PO.DAAC timeouts (likely sandbox rate-limit not site breakage), and two stray workspace files (one 0-byte, one .bak) that can be swept at next tinker pass. Overall: warn — none of the moderates are blocking; the decider can proceed normally. The other side of the argument — these moderates are recurring across runs without action; if no one ever picks them up the warn rating becomes meaningless. Recommend either fixing them on the next analyst/social pass or formally classifying them as wontfix with an issue ID, the way EXP-052 was handled.
